name: Krishna Janmabhoomi
description: The birthplace of Lord Krishna. A grand temple complex marking the exact spot where Krishna was born in a prison cell.

name: Banke Bihari Temple
description: One of the most revered temples in Vrindavan dedicated to Lord Krishna. Famous for its unique curtain darshan tradition.

name: Dwarkadhish Temple
description: 19th-century temple dedicated to Lord Krishna as the King of Dwarka. Known for elaborate Rajasthani architecture and vibrant festivals.

name: Vishram Ghat
description: The main ghat on the Yamuna where Lord Krishna is believed to have rested after slaying the tyrant Kansa. Evening aarti is mesmerizing.

name: Govardhan Hill
description: Sacred hill Lord Krishna lifted on his finger to protect villagers from Indra's rains. Pilgrims do parikrama (21 km circumambulation).

name: Prem Mandir
description: Stunning white marble temple in Vrindavan depicting Krishna's divine pastimes. Magnificent light show every evening.

name: ISKCON Vrindavan
description: Krishna-Balaram Mandir — ISKCON's most important temple in India. Beautiful deities, kirtans, and free vegetarian prasad daily.

name: Kusum Sarovar
description: Beautiful sandstone reservoir between Govardhan and Radha Kund. Features stunning chhatris and cenotaphs of Bharatpur rulers.

name: Radha Kund
description: Most sacred bathing place for Vaishnavas. Believed to have been created by Radha herself. Pilgrims bathe here on Ahoi Ashtami.

name: Nidhivan
description: Sacred grove where Krishna is believed to perform Raas Leela every night. The trees have unique twisted trunks. No one stays after sunset.

name: Keshi Ghat
description: Sacred ghat in Vrindavan where Krishna defeated the demon horse Keshi. Popular for sunrise prayers and peaceful Yamuna views.

name: Rangji Temple
description: Unique temple blending South Indian Dravidian, North Indian Rajput, and European Gothic architectural styles. A 50-foot gopuram stands tall.

name: Mathura Museum
description: Houses one of India's finest collections of Mathura School sculptures, Kushan-era art, and ancient coins dating back 2000 years.

name: Radha Vallabh Temple
description: 16th-century temple where Krishna's idol has no flute — its left hand is extended as if holding Radha's hand, symbolizing their eternal bond.

name: Radha Raman Temple
description: 500-year-old temple housing a self-manifested deity of Krishna. One of the most important Gaudiya Vaishnava temples.

name: Seva Kunj
description: Beautiful garden where Radha and Krishna are believed to have spent time together. Contains a small temple with divine murals.

name: Gita Mandir
description: Temple built by Birla family with entire Bhagavad Gita inscribed on its pillars. Features beautiful red sandstone architecture.

name: Potara Kund
description: Ancient stepped tank where baby Krishna's clothes were washed after his birth. A serene and historically significant site.

name: Jama Masjid
description: Built by Aurangzeb's governor in 1661, this mosque showcases fine Mughal architecture with four minarets and a large courtyard.

name: Birla Mandir
description: Modern marble temple built by the Birla family dedicated to Lakshmi-Narayan. Known for beautiful gardens and clean premises.
